description: Easy to perceive, understand, or see through; free from obstruction or confusion. 'Nuwi' names the quality of transparency—water one can see through, thoughts one can follow, communication without ambiguity.
sound_symbolism: The word sees through cleanly. 'Nu' begins with nasal openness, nothing blocking. Then 'wi' passes through, vision unobstructed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as adjective: 'nuwi phialu' (clear water). As verb: 'nuwi' (to clear, to clarify). Central to discussions of understanding, perception, and communication.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Clear mind is the goal of practice—perception undistorted by craving, aversion, or delusion.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Clear communication prevents misunderstanding—speaking so meaning passes through without distortion.
